text:
Sir Steve Redgrave has been announced for the new series of Dancing On Ice. The Olympian becomes the third star to be confirmed for the new series, after formerThe Only Way Is EssexstarFerne McCannandThe Traitorsicon Mollie Pearce were announced for the upcoming series.

Speaking onThis Morningon Monday, September 30, Steve revealed he had signed up for the show after making an ill-timed joke. He had recorded a video for show stars Torvill and Dean as they marked their 40th anniversary from their Olympics win as he had also won a medal that same four decades ago. At the time, he joked about them getting him on the show and that's exactly what they did.

He told hostsBen ShephardandCat Deeley: "People are rowers because they can't catch or throw. Rowing is a very coordinated sport, but it ends up with people not being very coordinated because they've done the same movements over and over again."

Training begins next week after a group masterclass on Tuesday, and it will be a few weeks on from that that Steve will meet his professional skating partner. He also revealed he is having a special pair of skates made to fit his size 12 feet.

OnGood Morning Britaintoday, Ferne McCann confirmed her reality show was ending, before adding her excitement about joining Dancing On Ice. She told hostsSusanna Reidand Ed Balls: "I'm doingDancing On Ice! So excited! I've done Winter Wonderland with the children, but the penguins the kids have got - I'm crunched over them!

"I'm looking forward to it; it's going to be a huge challenge. But the glitz and the glam, it's going to be brilliant."

The Traitors star Mollie Pearce was also announced for the new series after she lost out on the prize money on theBBCshow to friend Harry Clark, who betrayed her at the last moment. Speaking of joining Dancing On Ice during an appearance on ITV's Lorraine, she shared her hopes for the new series. She said: "It will be fun, I want to challenge myself, I'm ready for the challenge."

Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean are expected to return for the new series, which kicks off in January 2025. Oti Mabuse and Ashley Banjo are also likely to return as judges alongside the skating legends.

Last year, the show was hosted byHolly Willoughbyand Stephen Mulhern, who stepped in as a permanent host whenPhillip Schofieldexited the show. It has yet to be confirmed whether both Holly and Stephen will be back for the new series.

This Morning is on ITV1 weekdays at 10am and on ITVX

title: Sir Steve Redgrave signs up for Dancing On Ice as Olympic hero announced for 2025 series - The Mirror
author: Jasmine Allday
description: The Olympic rower has been announced for Dancing On Ice, after reality star Ferne McCann and The Traitors icon Mollie Pearce were confirmed for the skating show
